scope.out_of_scope_note: ~150+ cross-session uncommitted working-tree files are explicitly out-of-scope per dispatch; NOT tagged introduced_in_diff:true.
scope.baseline_used_for_introduced_in_diff: HEAD (931b547) ~ HEAD~5 (50f2ca2) — all five cycle-diff files have substantive deltas across this range per the dispatch's enumeration; the diffs that define this cycle's edits are present in HEAD
scope.introduced_in_diff_contract: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging per AC-12.1 iter-3; every finding object carries an explicit introduced_in_diff:bool. Absence of findings means no cleanliness violation was introduced by these 5 files.

per_file_audit.notes: Lives in canonical tests/generated/<test_run_id>/ dir alongside AC-01..AC-12 siblings. Filename test_AC_NN_<ac_uid>.py matches the project naming scheme (unique 16-hex ac_uid suffix prevents collision). Tested module agents/style-inspector.md exists. No empty pass-stub — contains real GIVEN/WHEN/THEN assertions. No backup/temp variant present.

Standard agent location agents/style-inspector.md is canonical. Agent is still referenced by /close (commands/close.md) and dispatch chain — NOT orphaned. The +2 lines (carve-out rewording and Cycle-2 mascot-rank exemption paragraph) are content edits within an existing actively-wired agent file; no file-organization-level cleanliness concern arises from the edit. The CJK content question (raised by codex F2) is a style-inspector concern, NOT a cleanliness-inspector concern, and is out-of-scope here.

Canonical hooks/doc_sync/ location. No backup/duplicate variant. Module is imported by hooks/doc_sync/sync.py (driver). New helper _is_skipped() is called from _build_stats() and _list_files() — not dead. SKIP_PREFIXES + dual-mode import are content edits within an existing wired module; semantic correctness of the import-fallback breadth (codex F3) is a code-review/QA concern, not file-organization cleanliness.

Canonical hooks/doc_sync/ location. No backup/duplicate variant. Module imported by hooks/doc_sync/sync.py. New helpers _matches_skip_prefix() and _is_dot_claude_specs() are both called inside build_tree() — not dead. Same out-of-scope notes as regen_readme.py for the import-fallback breadth.

Canonical scripts/ location, kebab-case matches convention. Sole variant — no blast-radius-tool-old.py / -backup.py present (verified via ls). Script is invoked by agents/ba.md and agents/qa.md per spec-20260518-225715 §5.3 (Phase 1 and Phase 2 entry points). All new internal callables (_try_add_edge, in_scope, severity_for, build_report, git_diff_files) are reachable from main()/build_report(). MAX_EDGES_PER_SOURCE_FILE constant is documented and exposed in output (codex F4 OBSERVATION_ONLY).

summary.rationale: Zero cleanliness violations introduced by the 5 cycle-diff files. All 5 files reside in canonical locations, follow project naming conventions, have no backup/temp/duplicate variants, contain no dead code, and remain functionally wired to their consumers. Per the AC-12.1 file-level scope contract and default-safe rules, file-level findings outside this 5-file scope (working-tree cross-session activity) are pre-existing/out-of-scope and do NOT block close.

ac_2_6_plumbing.default_safe_invocation: Per default-safe rule: in --changed-files mode this inspector returns ZERO findings; close.md MUST NOT escalate cleanliness to CLOSE: NO.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 findings (F1-F5). After role-scope classification: F1 (overbroad spec-2026 suppression) and F2 (CJK self-attesting exemption) are semantic-correctness concerns belonging to QA / style-inspector roles, NOT file-organization cleanliness scope; F3 (broad except ImportError) is a code-quality concern belonging to code review, NOT cleanliness scope; F4 was explicitly OBSERVATION_ONLY; F5 (baseline ambiguity for introduced_in_diff) is in-scope and was applied by recording the exact baseline range and adding a per-file introduced_in_diff tag.

findings.issue: F1 — Global spec-2026 basename suppression in hooks/doc_sync/{regen_readme,tree}.py is overbroad and could hide legitimate docs/dev/specs/spec-2026* docs from generated README/INDEX output.
findings.proposed_fix: Make spec-2026 suppression path-aware (e.g., only under .claude/specs/) and add a negative-control test asserting docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md remains renderable.
findings.rationale: Cleanliness-inspector scope = file organization, naming, archive candidates, duplicates, dead code, orphans. Semantic correctness of a leak-filter prefix (whether it over-suppresses) is a behavioral-correctness defect that belongs to QA-inspector (regression / test coverage of the leak filter) or to the dev/test-writer role, NOT to file-organization cleanliness. Per Rule 4 (requirement wins), the user's cleanliness requirement is to detect file-organization issues in the 5 cycle-diff files; the leak-filter scoping question is adjacent and is surfaced to the user as a separate concern rather than blocking close on a cleanliness verdict.

findings.issue: F2 — CJK in style-inspector.md line ~333 is only covered by the new mascot-rank exemption, which is partially self-attesting (defines and exercises its own exemption).
findings.proposed_fix: Tighten the exemption text to allow only the exact five-label allowlist at specific render sites; add a regression test that scans raw CJK hits and suppresses only those five labels.
findings.rationale: Whether a style exemption is self-attesting is the style-inspector role's concern (Standard 6 / CJK detection compliance), explicitly addressed by the style-inspector subagent which runs in parallel during /close. The cleanliness-inspector role does NOT adjudicate whether a doc's content correctly self-exempts from a style rule; that would be role-confusion. Surfaced to user for separate cycle if desired.

findings.issue: F3 — Broad except ImportError in dual-mode import fallback can mask real package import failures and mutate sys.path during normal package import.
findings.proposed_fix: Narrow the fallback: except ImportError: if __package__: raise; then perform the sys.path fallback.
findings.rationale: Exception-handler breadth and sys.path mutation safety are code-review / static-analysis concerns, not file-organization cleanliness. Cleanliness inspector does not flag code-style or robustness defects within a properly-located, non-dead, non-duplicate, properly-named file. Belongs to /code-review or a future QA pass.
Codex explicitly marked OBSERVATION_ONLY and confirmed this is not a violation; the constant is named, documented, and exposed in JSON output. No action.

findings.issue: F5 — Pre-existing-vs-NEW tagging ambiguity: SKIP_PREFIXES and MAX_EDGES_PER_SOURCE_FILE may pre-date the current cycle while the CJK exemption is fresh; tagging all five files uniformly as NEW could misrepresent the scope.
findings.proposed_fix: Record the exact diff base commit and derive introduced_in_diff per-finding from git diff <base>..HEAD.
findings.rationale: This is directly in cleanliness-inspector scope per AC-12.1 Mechanism (ii). Applied two ways: (1) added scope.baseline_used_for_introduced_in_diff field documenting the HEAD~5..HEAD range that this cycle's edits live in per the dispatch's explicit cycle-diff enumeration; (2) since findings count is ZERO, the per-finding introduced_in_diff:bool flag is moot — no finding can be miscategorized. The per_file_audit array records introduced_in_diff:true at the file granularity, meaning 'this file is in the cycle-diff scope as the dispatch declared'; this does NOT claim that every line in the file was newly added in this cycle. Default-safe rule preserved.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: F5 applied via explicit baseline documentation and introduced_in_diff tagging at file granularity; F1/F2/F3 rejected as out-of-role-scope (semantic / code-quality / style adjudication, not file-organization cleanliness) with rationale recorded; F4 logged as OBSERVATION_ONLY.
